programme_name: West Coast Groundfish Electronic Monitoring Program
organizations: NOAA Fisheries
objectives: Audit vessel logbook discards by EM data to ensure inseason catch accounting and compliance
quality_thresholds: Threshold is that video is completely unreviewable across all camera views. If haul not reviewable due to quality, move to next haul. T
review_objectives: Compliance monitoring; Catch accounting; protected species interactions
target_species_sampled: All taxa
bycatch_species_sampled: All taxa
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: Yes, only for some taxa/groups of taxa, with no limit of maximum number to be measured (please specify which in "Additional information")
technical_challenges: Video quality; data gaps; species identification
data_uses: Catch accounting; stock assessments; protected resources; enforcement
data_retention_limit:
raw data - EM Vendor 1 year after the close of the fishing year, NOAA Fisheries 5 years
processed - NOAA Fisheries indefinitely

programme_name: ETP species bycatch monitoring (Denmark)
organizations: DTU Aqua
additional_notes: Q.9 should allow for non-numerical responses in case the answer is an approximation. Also, 1. the number of vessels varies from one year to the next, and 2. the answer could depend if one takes all vessels registered or only the active ones. So, I guess you might want to be careful with the answers received.
objectives: ETP species bycatch assessment in GN
quality_thresholds: Yes, 4 levels depending on the certainty for a EM reviewer to identify the bycatch items.
technical_challenges: Sensor + cameras technical failures. Fishers not being collaborative (e.g. hiding the [by]catches from the camera view, not cleaning the lens regularly, etc.)
data_uses: DCF; research
data_retention_limit: Not currently.
ai_applications: Automatic detection of bycatch individuals (i.e. of things that are not target catches essentially); automatic detection of fishing operations; automatic blurring of stuff (for privacy issues)
ai_review_stage: Processing of sensor data;Processing of video data - catch review;GDPR-related issues;

programme_name: Marine Beacon - endangered species registration for AI

data_retention_limit: Yes

programme_name: Celtic Sea Scientific REM programme
organizations: Centre for Environment, Fisheries and Aquaculture Science (Cefas)
additional_notes: Only concentrating on OTB, not TBB or others
objectives: To generate detailed information on retained and discarded catches in the mixed demersal otter trawl fleet fishing in the Celtic Sea
sensor_transmission_frequency: Every 24-hour period if signal allows or at the end of each trip.
video_transmission_frequency: At the end of each trip
quality_thresholds: Hauls or potentially whole trips will be rejected for further analysis if: cameras are too dirty to accurately identify and quantify fish; 1 or more vital cameras are not working
review_objectives: Collect length information for selected species; quantify other species (retained and discarded) to enable to calculation of discard rates.
target_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
bycatch_species_sampled: All taxa
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: Yes, only for some taxa/groups of taxa, with a limit of maximum number to be measured (please specify which in "Additional information")
data_uses: The data is used to develop protocols for collecting data on these vessel types as well as answering R&D questions. A sufficient number of vessels has not been achieved to submit the data for use in stock assessments, but it has previously been used for national management/policy advice.
data_retention_limit: Not currently
ai_applications: For identifying and quantifying discards on conveyor belts and also fish stored in baskets.
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review

programme_name: REM and Regulation of Scallop Fishing SSI for scallop boats
organizations: Marine Directorate of Scottish Government
objectives: To ensure compliance with gear and spatial regulations. To generate improved information on distribution of fishing effort.
sensor_types: winch sensors
video_transmission_frequency: On demand only
programme_monitoring: Fishing activity on location i.e. confirmation of whether the vessel is fishing or not at a particular location.
review_objectives: Determining whether the gear is deployed or not, and how many dredges are deployed.
data_uses: Video and image data is only retained for enforcement action. Spatial data is kept for wider use in marine spatial planning and mapping of fishing effort.
data_retention_limit: Vessels are only required to retain data for one year from it's time of recording. There is no limit on how long the agency is retaining data.
ai_applications: To count the number of dredges deployed on each beam.
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - gear review

programme_name: Center for Fisheries Electronic Monitoring at Mote (CFEMM)
organizations: CFEMM - manages; organizations involved - NOAA NMFS Fisheries; Gulf of America Reef Fish Shareholders' Alliance; SouthEast Data, Assessment, and Review (SEDAR); Gulf Council; South Atlantic Council Stock Assessment Advisory committee; independent vessel owners, captains, and crew; New England Marine Monitoring
areas_of_operation: Gulf of America; Southeastern Atlantic off Florida
video_recording_type: Interrupted according to sensor data (e.g. video recording is triggered by winch operation; specify in the additional information field)
quality_thresholds: Yes, complete event (set, soak, and haul)
review_objectives: bycatch; discards; condition
sampling_coverage_primary: 25% for BLL, 100% VL; can varying depending on funded project objectives
target_species_sampled: All taxa
bycatch_species_sampled: All taxa
species_id_resolution: Same level for all taxa and groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: No
sex_collected: Yes, for some taxa/groups of taxa for which this is possible externally (and specify in the field for comments)
additional_characteristics: condition brought onboard; fate; condition if discarded; estimated size of sharks based on 3 size ranges
technical_challenges: voluntary program, captains not turning on systems prior to sets or at beginning of trip; dirty camera lens; certain camera brands leaking; reliable power on vessels
data_uses: Provide individual captains/owner data products back to them; Grouped data products to fishing vessel organizations for overview and to fulfill specific data requests for their input to the Gulf Council; Data included in working papers (9) to SouthEast Data, Assessment, and Review (SEDAR); specific Gulf Council data requests; Data products to fulfill reporting to funders such as the National Fish and Wildlife Foundation and NOAA Fisheries; Presentations at American Fisheries Society, World Fisheries Congress, and other scientific and general community meetings.
data_retention_limit: No, We have all data since 2016, data not reviewed is archived for future use
ai_applications: Fish presence; fish ID
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review

programme_name: Voluntary camera-documented fisheries in Kattegat
organizations: Danish Agricultural and Fisheries Agency; Danish Fishers PO
additional_notes:
Programme is extension of the Kattegat-project, which Denmark has run since 2021. Reports for that project are available at the Danish Agricultural and Fisheries Agency webpage:
https://lfst.dk/erhvervsfiskeri/kontrol/monitering-og-overvaagning/kameradokumenteret-fiskeri/evalueringsrapporter
objectives: The purpose of the EM in the Kattegat is to document the Nephrops fishery, in particular in terms of bycatch of cod and compliance with the landing obligation.
video_transmission_frequency: 5 percent sampling + unlimited risk based. Video from trips selected for control are requested before next trip, usually one day after the end of the trip
video_recording_type: Interrupted according to sensor data (e.g. video recording is triggered by winch operation; specify in the additional information field)
quality_thresholds: 5 percent sampling (maximum)
review_objectives: Control of landing obligation (although not possible for all species)
target_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
bycatch_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: Yes, only for some taxa/groups of taxa, with no limit of maximum number to be measured (please specify which in "Additional information")
additional_characteristics: Assesment of severity level when non-compliance is observed (intentional or unintentional)
technical_challenges: Obstruction of camera view, work procedure ; Water droplets on lens ; sun glare; low light; Smudge on lenses ; Obstruction of camera view due to masking being askew ; Rotation sensors prone to breakdown
data_uses: Control and science (regulation allow for transfer of data to science use, not video data though)
data_retention_limit: 1 month for video in the Agency. 1 year for data on the vessel BlackBox camera system. No limit for data when case for non-compliance is being processed and prosecuted. No limit for catch quantification data and sensor data
ai_applications: Species recognition
ai_review_stage: Processing of sensor data;Annotation of images
ai_developer: Anchor Lab and Danish Agricultural and Fisheries Agency

programme_name: DNK EM – ETP species bycatch sampling
organizations: DTU Aqua
areas_of_operation: ICES 4a; ICES 4b; ICES 3a20; ICES 3a21; ICES 3b23; ICES 3c22;
sensor_transmission_frequency: after the end of each trip
video_transmission_frequency: after the end of each trip
image_capture_config: Manually, when a bycatch occurs, a single image is saved
video_selection_method: Census eventually, but since it can take time to get there, we first review at least one haul / fishing day
quality_thresholds: yes
review_objectives: Linked to specific research project (i.e. not all the data are reviewed for catches)
target_species_sampled: One/some group/s of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
bycatch_species_sampled: All taxa

length_measurements: No
sex_collected: Yes, for some taxa/groups of taxa for which this is possible externally (and specify in the field for comments)
additional_characteristics: Age or maturity when there is sexual or age-related dimorphism
technical_challenges: Quality of the raw imagery data; Fishers obstructing the view (willingly or not) or turning off the EMS
data_uses: DCF; research projects
data_retention_limit: no
ai_applications: Automatic identification (and later on classification) of ETP species; Blurring of humans and markings; Fishing effort detection from geospatial data only (not using imagery data, but "just" GPS/GNSS data)
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review;Processing of sensor data

programme_name: Electronic monitoring in the Danish Kattegat (3AS) Nephrops fishery
organizations: Danish Agricultural and Fisheries Agency
additional_notes:
Three reports have been published for the project:
1) Electronic monitoring in the Danish Kattegat (3AS) Nephrops fishery - Evaluation on phase 1 of the project 
2) Electronic monitoring in the Danish Kattegat (3AS) Nephrops fishery - Full evaluation of the project
3) Electronic monitoring by the Danish fisheries control, 2021 - 2024
objectives: The main purpose of using electronic monitoring with camera documentation in the Nephrops fishery in the Kattegat has been to demonstrate compliance with the landing obligation and to assess electronic monitoring as a control tool.
sensor_types: rotation sensor (winch); motion detection; depth for some vessels when possible
video_transmission_frequency: Upon request. Benchmark of 5 percent sampling of trips for control
video_recording_type: Interrupted according to sensor data (e.g. video recording is triggered by winch operation; specify in the additional information field)
quality_thresholds: Video quality graded from 0-10, where 0 is no video and 10 is perfect. Video quality should be 7 in order to be acceptable as a basis
review_objectives: Compliance with the landing obligation
target_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
bycatch_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: Yes, only for some taxa/groups of taxa, with no limit of maximum number to be measured (please specify which in "Additional information")
technical_challenges: Obstruction of camera view, work procedure; Sun glare; Water droplets on lens;  Low or no light;  Smudge on lenses; Obstruction of camera view due to masking being askew ; Rotation sensors prone to breakdown
data_uses: AI training material
data_retention_limit: 1 month for video at Agency, 1 year for data onboard vessels, unlimited for sensordata and catch quantification as well as processed data (including images)
ai_applications: Species recognition
ai_image_subjects: One image of every single registry, mainly fish and mainly whiting and haddock. Roughly 400,000 images in total
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review

programme_name: Trial fishery with EM for sandeel in the Baltic Sea 2022 and 2023
organizations: Danish Fisheries Agency; DTU Aqua  National Institute of Aquatic Resources
additional_notes:
From evaluation report:

The main purpose with the trials was to avoid cod catches in the sandeel fishery and the EM system
was placed to document this. The sandeel fisheries were done with trawl and catches were taken
onboard in bulk where they went straight into the cargo hold for landing. In general, it was easier
to identify species than first thought, as larger fish such as cod and whiting (bycatch) tended to
“float” on top of the sandeel. In addition, the catch sorting process was short as the catch simply
slided into the cargo hold. Video reviewers would replay videos at down to 0.25 times the speed to
see bycatches but as catch came in during the beginning of the catch coming in and because the
review only needed to go through roughly 40 minutes of catch handling on average, this was
manageable
objectives: Trial fishery to test avoidance of cod catches in the sandeel fishery. EM system was placed to document catches
sensor_types: rotation sensor; motion detection (in cameras)
video_transmission_frequency: Upon request from Danish Fisheries Agency
video_recording_type: Interrupted according to sensor data (e.g. video recording is triggered by winch operation; specify in the additional information field)
quality_thresholds: Min. 10% review of trips
review_objectives: Estimation of bycatch, mainly cod
target_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
bycatch_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: Yes, only for some taxa/groups of taxa, with no limit of maximum number to be measured (please specify which in "Additional information")
additional_characteristics: Onboard sampling (assesment of wether the sampling is objective or if subjective sampling is taking place (not legal))
technical_challenges: None in this trial
data_uses: No current. Was used to asses the bycatch level of cod in a closed area
data_retention_limit: 1 month for video at Agency, 1 year for data onboard vessels, unlimited for sensordata and catch quantification as well as processed data (including images)
ai_applications: None
ai_review_stage: Not

programme_name: Trial fishery with EM, targeting greater weever in the Kattegat
organizations: Danish Agricultural and Fisheries Agency; Danish Fishers PO; DTU Aqua – National Institute of Aquatic Resources
additional_notes: The trial fishery with EM targetting greater weever ran for two years and as two seperate projects. However, in term of EM, the trials were the same and have therefore filled in as for one project running for two years
objectives: The purpose of the trial was to assess whether a fishery targeting greater weever could be sustainable. Part of the trial fishery was a protocol for sampling of catches as well as self-reporting. The EM was used to verify that these requirements were met and to document bycatches
sensor_types: winch rotation sensors; motion detection (in cameras)
video_transmission_frequency: Upon request after the end of fishing trip selected for control
video_recording_type: Interrupted according to sensor data (e.g. video recording is triggered by winch operation; specify in the additional information field)
quality_thresholds: Min. 10 % of trips must be controlled
review_objectives: Catch documentation; verification of logbooks; assessment of whether sampling protocol has been followed
target_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
bycatch_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: Yes, only for some taxa/groups of taxa, with no limit of maximum number to be measured (please specify which in "Additional information")
additional_characteristics: Sampling procedure is assessed (not species, but whether sampling is following protocol)
technical_challenges: Obstruction of camera view due to work procedure; Water droplets on lens; Smudge on lenses; Obstruction of camera view due to masking being askew; Rotation sensors prone to breakdown; Difficulty in discerning bycatch (eg. cod or whiting) from target species (greater weever)
data_uses: Assessment of whether a new fishery for greater weever is sustainable
data_retention_limit: 1 year for data onboard vessels; unlimited for sensordata and catch quantification as well as processed data (including images)
ai_applications: Bycatch species recognition
ai_image_subjects: Individual bycatch (eg. whiting) among greater weever (target species)
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review

programme_name: Fully Documented Fisheries (FDF)
organizations: Danish Pelagic Producers Organisation; DTU Aqua; Aalborg University; Danish Agricultural and Fisheries Agency
target_species: Herring; Mackerel; Sprat; Sandeel; Blue Whiting; Norway Pout; Horse Mackerel
objectives: Documenting compliance with the landing obligation and using the camera data for scientific purposes such as catch composition estimation, size estimation, bycatch of ETP species etc.
sensor_types: Data from the RSW tanks will be implemented
video_transmission_frequency: Live view possible but high quality data upload after each trip or via Starlink
quality_thresholds: No
review_objectives: Compliance with landing obligation
target_species_sampled: All taxa
bycatch_species_sampled: All taxa
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: No
technical_challenges: Video quality being sub-optimal and the flow of fish being pumped unto the dewatering boxes being very fast.
data_uses:
1. Control for compliance of landing obligation
2. Scientific purposes for catch estimation and ETP species bycatch (anomaly detection)
data_retention_limit:
The agreement with the control authorities is that they can ask for data going 1 maximum year back

Data can be stored longer if needed.
ai_applications: Detecting when hauling and pumping on the vessel (activity based); Anomaly detection; Catch estimation
ai_image_subjects: Anomalies such as ETP species and other bycatch species
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review
ai_developer: Integrated Monitoring; Aalborg University, DTU Aqua

organizations: HLA

review_objectives: Count the fish; count the PET events; classify the fish, classify the PET species; monitor gear; Help the fishers fish more efficiently; Aide the vessel owners in knowing how many fish (think money) they have on the water.

bycatch_species_sampled: One/some group/s of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")

ai_applications: catch detection; PET detection; fish species classification; fish sizing; gear detection; fishing behavior

programme_name: The development of Electronic Monitoring and Reporting (EMR) technology for fisheries in Portugal (EMREP)
organizations: EEA Grants; OLSPS Int; OLSPS Marine; Universidade do Algarve; IPMA; Imenco SA; Crustaceo fishing company.
additional_notes: It was a pilot study with main focus on development and deployment of the integrated iEMR technology. It was not tested in the context of actual impact on the fishery.
objectives: To integrate electronic reporting and monitoring technologies using as a case study deep sea elasmobranchs as bycatch
video_transmission_frequency: after the end of each trip
video_recording_type: Interrupted according to instructions provided to the vessel operators (please give details in the additional information field)
image_capture_config: Can be done by the skipper or the researcher on board when ever something important is spotted.
quality_thresholds: Image must be clear without fractals.
review_objectives: Count and identify sharks and skates species.
bycatch_species_sampled: One/some group/s of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: No
additional_characteristics: diversity of elasmobranchs; number of individuals
technical_challenges: At start camera configuration was freezing the images, therefore some hauls were lost.
data_uses:
Development of technical paper with summary of the results with special focus on
1. technical viability of the iEMR system 
2. assessment of how affective is the system as a bycatch monitoring tool for elasmobranchs 
3. assessment of how affective is the system as a bycatch monitoring tool for the landing bligation
data_owner: Shared between the technology provider and the research and development institution
data_retention_limit: According to the funding program the data is store for 5 years, but it will be stored indefinitely.
ai_applications: Elasmobranchs identification
ai_review_stage: Technical practicality in the context of real time field operation.

programme_name: Opportunities for Artificial Intelligence in Data Processing and Storage for Fisheries
organizations: Ai.Fish LLC; National Fish and Wildlife Foundation (NFWF); Pacific Islands Fisheries Science Center (PIFSC); Vessel Management Associates; Google LLC; Amazon Web Services; Microsoft Azure; Hawaii Technology Development Corporation; Various volunteer contributors; EM service providers such as Teem.Fish, SnapIT, Archipelago.
target_species: Albacore tuna; Yellowfin tuna; Skipjack tuna; Swordfish
fleet_size_total: Approx. 145 in deep-set tuna fishery; 18 in shallow-set swordfish fishery
additional_notes: Observers onboard approx. 20% of deep-set, 100% of shallow-set trips; integration with existing EM systems onboard vessels.
objectives: Improve efficiency and accuracy of EM video review; develop and deploy machine learning and computer vision software; reduce manual review time by 75%.
hardware_provider: Various EM hardware providers onboard vessels; Ai.Fish focused on software
sensor_types: Video cameras; GPS receivers; hydraulic sensors; drum rotation sensors
video_transmission_frequency: end of each trip
data_uses: Fish species classification; trip monitoring; regulatory reporting assistance; improving EM review efficiency
ai_applications: computer vision
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review

programme_name: Smart Pass Phase II – SmartPass Computer Vision for Fisheries at the Edge​
organizations: Ai.Fish; Environmental Defense Fund (EDF)​
areas_of_operation: Newport, Oregon, USA (harbor entrance); specific to pilot site
additional_notes: Boat-based estimates of catch; focus is monitoring vessel activity using AI​

review_objectives: boat-based catch estimation​
data_uses: Compare human and AI vessel/boat counts, technology demonstration, system performance analysis​

ai_applications: Boat detection, count estimation, algorithm benchmarking​
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review;Processing of video data - quality assurance

programme_name: Operationalizing Machine Learning in Alaska's Fixed Gear Electronic Monitoring Program
organizations: Ai.Fish LLC; Archipelago Marine Research; Alaska Fixed Gear EM Program; National Fish and Wildlife Foundation (NFWF); Microsoft Azure; Various commercial fishery stakeholders
target_species: Alaska groundfish species; species handled by fixed gear fisheries
areas_of_operation: Alaska coastal waters and federal management areas
additional_notes: Data collected from 16 trips from these vessels
objectives: Deploy machine learning processing for EM video review; reduce data review time by up to 50%; demonstrate AI-assisted review feasibility in Alaska fisheries; improve review accuracy
hardware_provider: fisheries operated EM hardware; Ai.Fish provides software
sensor_types: bounding boxes for fish, humans, gear floats annotated
quality_thresholds: Low-quality video (e.g., obscured frames, bad lighting) excluded or downweighted during review
review_objectives: Fish detection and classification; catch count accuracy; reduce manual review time through AI assistance
sampling_coverage_primary: 16 trips monitored; actual sampling coverage not expressed as percentage overall fleet
sampling_coverage_secondary: Coverage of video segments for training and testing exceeded plan
sampling_coverage_tertiary: All detected fish in sampled clips annotated/verified
target_species_sampled: One/some group/s of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
bycatch_species_sampled: One/some group/s of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: No
additional_characteristics: Bounding boxes for gear floats and humans to assist fish detection models
technical_challenges: Occluded fish in “piles”; video upload speed limitations; camera placement affects fish visibility; human reviewers occasionally uncertain
data_uses: Automated catch detection processing; AI-assisted video review; training data for model improvements
data_retention_limit: no
ai_applications: Automated fish detection; catch counting; quality assurance; reviewer workload reduction

programme_name: Multimodal Computer Vision System for Fisheries Monitoring (NOAA SBIR Phase I)

review_objectives: Fish detection; object tracking; counting; species classification
sampling_coverage_primary: Approximately 20 vessels across regions sampled; split into training and evaluation sets
sampling_coverage_secondary: Large number of clips (>1.8 million annotations)

bycatch_species_sampled: One/some group/s of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")

ai_image_subjects: Video frames of fish, people, gear from fishing vessels

programme_name: AI-Assisted Electronic Monitoring Solutions for Groundfish Trawl in the Pacific
organizations: Ai.Fish LLC; Fisheries and Oceans Canada
target_species: Pacific groundfish (dominant and secondary groups: rockfish, flatfish, cod, sole )
vessel_size_range: Not specified for pilot. DFO groundfish trawl fleet typically ~70 vessels
additional_notes: Analysis focused on catch apportionment during each tow; reviewers estimated catch by visually assessing proportion of species groups in video
objectives: Reduce human reviewer workload/time per tow   Increase accuracy and consistency in catch apportionment   Support bilingual access for Canadian operations   Facilitate technology transfer to commercial workflow
hardware_provider: Existing trawl camera systems administered by DFO and partners
sensor_transmission_frequency: Per tow; video reviewed after each tow event
video_transmission_frequency: end of each trip
quality_thresholds: Reviewers screened for visible catch area and sorting belt clarity in video; multi-camera option for higher visibility
review_objectives: Improve consistency and performance of catch apportionment; reduce training burden for junior reviewers

bycatch_species_sampled: One/some group/s of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
species_id_resolution: Same level for all taxa and groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")

data_retention_limit: standard DFO research period
ai_applications: Automate dominant/minor species grouping per tow event

sensor_transmission_frequency: Per trip via manual offload; no real-time transmission
video_transmission_frequency: after the end of each trip
quality_thresholds: Video gaps and camera failures limited some reviews; poor visibility acknowledged
review_objectives: with focus on discard and bycatch monitoring; Comprehensive catch and discard identification; review protocol adherence

programme_name: Swedish small-scale fishery bycatch monitoring
organizations: Swedish University of Agricultural Science (SLU); Swedish Agency for Marine and Water Management
target_species: Flatfishes; herring; perch; whitefish; mixed fish
additional_notes: Trip duration in our case refers to soak time.
objectives: The main objective is to monitor at least 5% of the the total fishing effort of the small-scale net fishery in Sweden for instances of bycatch of ETP species.
sensor_transmission_frequency: After every fishing operation given that the system is running. Otherwise data is transferred the next time the system is turned on.
video_transmission_frequency: After every fishing operation given that the system is running. Otherwise data is transferred the next time the system is turned on.
quality_thresholds: All video footage collected (~400-600 per year) are reviewed. There is no quality threshhold, but each video(camera) is tagged for video quality on a scale (1-6)
review_objectives: Observing bycatch events; marking haul operations; indicating video quality; noting instances where the "drop-out zone" is not visible
technical_challenges: Video quality: darkness, weather, dirty lenses etc; lag in playback; camera angles.
data_uses: The data collected is used to estimate bycatch rates of ETP-species. So far the main focus has been bycatch of harbour propoises.
data_retention_limit: Yes. Video footage is only stored for a maximum of 90 days after it reaches SLU ervers. After the 90 day period, or when the data has been reviewed and gone though quality checks, it is deleted.
ai_applications: Masking out personal information (people and text)
ai_review_stage: Masking (AI) is applied to the footage before storage and review. No raw, unmasked material is stored.

programme_name: Test study 1: Developing electronic monitoring methods of bycatch in Finnish fisheries

programme_name: Remote Electronic Monitoring (REM) pilot project for monitoring Fishing Activities of Cyprus Fishing vessels
organizations: Department of Fisheries and Marine Research
sensor_types: Light;Door;Winch
video_transmission_frequency: On demand
data_uses: The Cyprus Department of Fisheries and Marine Research has chosen the use of Remote Electronic Monitoring (REM) including CCTV technology as a control and monitoring tool to improve compliance and enforcement of the LO implementation.  The aim of this pilot project and the use of REM technology is the prevention of overfishing and illegal discarding practices, guaranteeing a long-term sustainability.

programme_name: VISIM
organizations: ILVO
objectives: To implement a camera-based system as a complement to manual sampling in the Belgian beam trawl fishery, increasing data flow on catches and discards, improving coverage of less frequent species, and supporting science through AI-based image analysis.
sensor_types: GPS; Gear sensors; Hydraulic sensors; Winch activity sensors
video_transmission_frequency: After each trip
quality_thresholds: Basic quality checks are applied (e.g. sufficient lighting, visibility of catch, limited occlusion, camera stability)
review_objectives: To quantify catches and discards; To improve data completeness and temporal coverage; To enhance detection of less frequent and data-poor species; To support scientific analysis and stock assessment; To develop and validate AI-based image analysis methods
target_species_sampled: One/some group/s of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
bycatch_species_sampled: One/some group/s of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")

technical_challenges: Occlusion and overlapping fish; Variable lighting conditions; Motion blur; Water and debris on lens; Species similarity and misclassification; Variability in fish orientation and partial visibility
data_uses: Method development; Testing and validation of AI-based image analysis; Exploration of catch and discard data; Supporting scientific research
data_retention_limit: Yes;, raw data storage is limited and depends on project agreements and storage capacity, processed data may be stored longer for scientific analysis and method development

programme_name: Trawl EM Program
organizations: National Marine Fisheries Service
areas_of_operation: Bering Sea, Aleutian Islands, Gulf of Alaska
trip_duration: 1-3 days during Jan - April; 3-5 during June - Oct.
additional_notes: Limited to pelagic trawl gear; 99.8% target species. Primary concern is salmon (prohibited species in this program)
objectives: (1) improve salmon accounting; (2) reduce monitoring costs; and (3) improve the quality of monitoring data. This program is a compliance monitoring program focused on maximizing retention of catch.
sensor_types: Hydraulic, GPS
sensor_transmission_frequency: GPS at 10 second interval; Hydraulic constantly
video_transmission_frequency: Every 5 trips
quality_thresholds: Yes; Reviewers must be able to see events on the deck clearly.
review_objectives: Ensure compliance with retention requirements.
target_species_sampled: All taxa
bycatch_species_sampled: All taxa
species_id_resolution: Same level for all taxa and groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: Yes, for all taxa/groups of taxa, with a limit of maximum number to be measured (please specify which in "Additional information")
sex_collected: Yes, for all taxa/groups of taxa for which this is possible externally
technical_challenges: General image quality issues. Some technical issues with specific systems.
data_uses: Video data is reviewed to note any discards while the vessel is at sea.
data_retention_limit: No; We mandate 3 years of retention, however data could be kept longer if needed.
ai_applications: Discard detection
ai_review_stage: In development

programme_name: California Dungeness Crab
organizations: CDFW; PSMFC; Archipelago
objectives: Marine Spatial Planning; Whale Entanglement Mitigation
sensor_types: Hydraulic Pressure
data_uses: Marine Spatial Planning; Cetacean entanglement risk.
ai_applications: Sensor-based Effort
ai_review_stage: Processing of sensor data

programme_name: Audit Model Program (U.S. Northeast Fisheries)
organizations: NOAA Fisheries, National Marine Fisheries Service
areas_of_operation: Groundfish are distributed throughout the Greater Atlantic region, from Cape Hatteras, North Carolina, to the U.S./Canada border.  The majority of the groundfish that are landed in the Greater Atlantic Region are harvested in the Gulf of Maine and on Georges Bank.
flag_states: Northeast Unites States: Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Maine, New Hampshire
objectives: Amendment 23 adjusts the industry-funded at-sea monitoring program for groundfish sectors to improve the accuracy of collected catch data (landings and discards) and catch accounting.
hardware_provider: Integrated Monitoring ;New England Marine Monitoring ;Saltwater Inc.;Teem Fish Monitoring
data_transmission_primary: Physical receipt (hard drives);Remote upload - automatic;Remote upload - on-demand
sensor_types: Skymate
sensor_transmission_frequency: Every hour for VMS (Skymate) and other sensor data varies by EM service providers.
video_transmission_frequency: Varies, but typically after each trip or at the end of a fishing week.
quality_thresholds: No.  It's a random census of all trips (video) regardless of video/system performance.  This helps the agency keep track of how well the system is functioning and when there are problems are those reported.
review_objectives: The main objectives are to monitor discards (species identification and length estimation) to support fish quota management.
sampling_coverage_primary: Experienced vessels are selected at 35% of total trips and new vessels are selected at a rate of 50%.
sampling_coverage_secondary: All trips go through data audits, but only 50% of the total trips go through a more intensive review comparing the video and summarized data supplied by the service provider.
bycatch_species_sampled: Specific taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: Yes, only for some taxa/groups of taxa, with no limit of maximum number to be measured (please specify which in "Additional information")
technical_challenges: Internet speed and streaming video.  Occasionally the video appears choppy and pixelated.
data_uses: Data is primarily used to support quota management.  As a secondary measure, some data (length) is used for science and stock assessment purposes.
data_retention_limit: Yes.  The fishing industry is required to hold the data for 1 year after the fishing season officially closes.  NOAA Fisheries holds the data for 5 years.
ai_applications: The identification and length estimation of fish.
ai_review_stage: Processing of sensor data;Processing of video data - catch review
ai_developer: The agency is using CVision AI.  Most of the service providers are pursuing AI on their own for various reasons as well.

programme_name: Option A Trawl
organizations: DFO, Canadian Groundfish Research and Conservation Society, Vericatch
primary_contact: Lindsay Richardson Lindsay.Richardson-Deranger@dfo-mpo.gc.ca; Theresa Williams theresa@pfmibc.ca; Ross River ross@vericatch.com
additional_notes: A few Option A Trawl vessels will also fish in the Hake Trawl fishery.
objectives: EM sensor and video data collected for entire trip. EM data reviewed internally. All tows viewed for total catch weight. 10% wet boat and 25% RTV tows are viewed for species composition. EM and DMP data is audited against skipper log data and sent to DFO with Occurrence Reports for enforcement.
sensor_types: Hydraulic; Rotation
sensor_transmission_frequency: GPS once per ten seconds ; Hydraulic every time hydraulics for fishing gear (trawl net) are engaged ; Rotation every time the net drum rotates
video_transmission_frequency: After the end of each trip
quality_thresholds: Tows can only be reviewed if they contain video gaps less than 4 consecutive mins in duration.
programme_monitoring: The compliance of commercial trawl groundfish fishing vessels in Canadian Pacific waters.
review_objectives: Noting the occurrence of fishing in closed areas for all fishing activity; All tows viewed for total catch weight estimate ; 10% wet boat and 25% RTV tows are viewed for species composition; processed EM and DMP data is audited against skipper log data and sent to DFO with Occurrence Reports for enforcement.
technical_challenges: Poor camera views due to foggy or water splattered camera dome, sun glare and/or poor lighting; Poor camera placement; Data gaps
data_uses: Compliance; Science (stock assessment)
data_retention_limit: Raw hard drive data is held for 14 days starting from the date the trip audit is sent to DFO and only if the audit passes and there are no holds placed on the trip. Raw hard drive data from trips with failed audits and/or other holds are held until released by DFO. Sensor data and emi files from EM data review in Interpret are stored in Archipelago's data management system (Portal) indefinitely.
ai_applications: Event classification (haul detection) ; Conveyor based fish counting and speciation; length measurement
ai_image_subjects: On deck activity ; gear operation ; below deck conveyor ; catch sorting and measuring
ai_review_stage: Exploration and testing

programme_name: Clean Catch UK
organizations: Fishing Industry ; BMP; SMRU; Arribada Initiative; MMOC; Cefas; ZSL; Mindfully Wired
target_species: Hake; Turbot; Dover sole; Bass; Crawfish; Monk; Mackerel
objectives: REM in the Clean Catch pinger trial is used to independently record pinger presence and bycatch occurrence, in order to validate skipper self‑reporting, support estimation of bycatch rates, and assess whether pingers reduce bycatch, particularly of common dolphins.
sensor_types: latitude, longitude; speed; date; time
sensor_transmission_frequency: After the end of each trip
video_transmission_frequency: After the end of each trip
image_capture_config: Still images are extracted from the recorded video footage during the analysis phase
quality_thresholds: n/a
programme_monitoring: Bycatch occurrence and species composition of sensitive taxa, including cetaceans, seals, seabirds, and elasmobranchs. As well as noting if acoustic deterrent devices (pingers) are deployed.
review_objectives: Confirm pinger presence and bycatch occurrence at the haul level.
technical_challenges: During video and image review, common technical challenges include variable video quality caused by low light, glare, turbidity and camera obstruction, along with occasional system faults such as recording gaps or data transmission issues.
data_uses: Besides the pinger trial, the data is used for BMP stock assessment and development of AI to identify bycatch.
data_retention_limit: n/a
ai_applications: To identify and estimate bycatch species and events
ai_image_subjects: Birds, cetaceans, seals, elasmobranchs, and skates and rays.
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review

programme_name: GHLCMP
organizations: DFO Pacific; DFO National; BC Commercial Industry Groundfish Society (BCCIGS); industry members - fishers, fish plants, fish buyers, vessel owners; International Pacific Halibut Commission (IPHC)
primary_contact: Katie Archer katiea@archipelago.ca; Scott Buchanan scottb@archipelago.ca
objectives: sustainable fishery proof, 100% monitoring and audited skipper data and catch accountability, quota management data collection, fishery effort, effort per unit catch
sensor_types: optionally drum rotation and hyraulic pressure
sensor_transmission_frequency: varies depending if Vantage connected to Fleet transmitting prior to offload or V5/V4 only at hard drive collection
video_transmission_frequency: after the end of each trip
video_recording_type: Interrupted according to sensor data (e.g. video recording is triggered by winch operation; specify in the additional information field)
quality_thresholds: Yes, not more than 4 minutes of video can be deemed unusable, if this threshold is met then trip fails audit, is reported to DFO, vessel cannot go out fishing again until issue is resolved to DFO's satisfaction, and new set is randomly selected to view
review_objectives: enumeration of catch by piece count and species; regulatory compliance; catch and effort by fishing area and time period
sampling_coverage_secondary: up to 100% review at the discretion and direction of DFO if an audit has been failed
target_species_sampled: All taxa
bycatch_species_sampled: All taxa
species_id_resolution: Different levels for different taxa/groups of taxa (please specify which in "Additional information")
length_measurements: Yes, only for some taxa/groups of taxa, with no limit of maximum number to be measured (please specify which in "Additional information")
technical_challenges: Lighting; weather; time of day; water on lens; angle of camera; position of crew obstructing view
data_uses: Fisher education; Regulatory compliance monitoring, reporting, and response/prosecution; Quota management;
data_retention_limit: raw data held for 14 days following release of quota status report unless held for another reason; images and video clips may be held indefinitely on Archipealgo's Portal and/or other locations
ai_applications: faster review of catch
ai_review_stage: Processing of video data - catch review
